setup_task.sh lines 20-24 defines COLOR CODING SCHEME with these exact hex colors for User-Facing, Business Logic, Platform, and Data
Specification requires 18 components as rounded rectangles. Starter diagram has ~15 shapes (axes, title, labels). Total expected >30 shapes.
setup_task.sh lines 47-68 specify 24 dependency edges (from DEPENDENCIES section) + 6 evolution arrows (from EVOLUTION ARROWS section) = 30 total new edges. Metadata min_edges=15 is conservative.
Official GitHub release verified at https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop/releases/tag/v26.0.9 released January 27 with Electron 32.2.5

privileged_info_summary: The wardley_map_cloud_migration task is a synthetic, specification-driven creative task where an agent must manually create a Wardley Map diagram in draw.io. The specification defines exactly 18 fintech components (Customer Mobile App, Payment Processing, KYC/AML Engine, etc.) to be positioned on a 2D value chain/evolution axis with specific coordinates (given as percentages). The task requires 4 color codes (#4A90D9 Blue, #7AB648 Green, #F5A623 Orange, #9B59B6 Purple) for four categories (User-Facing, Business Logic, Platform, Data). 24 dependency edges must be drawn as solid arrows plus 6 evolution arrows as dashed lines, totaling 30 new edges beyond the 5 base axes. A PDF export to /home/ga/Diagrams/exports/wardley_map_cloud_migration.pdf is required. All data is synthetically generated by setup_task.sh; no external datasets are used. The task uses draw.io desktop version 26.0.9 with AppImage installation.
